sources.notes: Range queries over palindrome-related string pairs.
Official editorial update says the standard solution is Theta(n log^2 n + q log n) due to relevant pairs of disjoint palindromic substrings.

annotation.summary: Answer range queries involving pairs/combinations of palindromic substrings.
annotation.constraints: n and q are large; all palindromic information must be preprocessed and queried logarithmically.
annotation.core_idea: Precompute palindromic substrings or palindromic centres using standard string techniques, then reduce each query to counting relevant pairs of disjoint palindromic substrings inside the interval. The nontrivial part is that there are only O(n log n) relevant pairs after filtering by containment/dominance. Store those pairs in offline data structures so each query becomes a logarithmic 2D/range counting query.
annotation.complexity: Theta(n log^2 n + q log n).

solution_variants.name: offline-palindrome-pair-counting
solution_variants.summary: Generate relevant palindrome interval pairs and answer queries with offline range/dominance data structures.
solution_variants.complexity: Theta(n log^2 n + q log n)

tags.evidence: The core objects are palindromic substrings and their interval relations.
Offline dominance/range counting over palindrome pairs is naturally implemented with Fenwick trees or segment trees.
Queries are answered after generating and sorting relevant palindrome interval pairs.
